reasoning: The Lean signature encodes exactly the informal claim. `[MetricSpace X] [CompleteSpace X] [Nonempty X]` captures "nonempty complete metric space". The conclusion `∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ G n` is equivalent to ⋂ₙ Gₙ being nonempty, matching exactly what the informal statement asks (the weaker form — nonemptiness, not density of the intersection). No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the types/quantifier structure align.
